condition: [Patch rule — bronzed_cowbird_vs_shiny_cowbird] Bird is entirely glossy black plumage (male plumage; no brown tones visible); Iris is clearly red or ruby-red in color — not dark brown or black; Bill is visibly thick and heavy at the base, with a slightly decurved culmen, appearing proportionally deeper than a typical Shiny Cowbird bill; No obvious iridescent purple-blue gloss dominating the head and body (Shiny Cowbird shows strong blue-purple iridescence; Bronzed shows greener-bronze gloss); Bird shows a distinctly inflated or ruff-like appearance around the neck and upper breast — the prominent neck ruff (hackles) characteristic of Bronzed Cowbird is visible or at least not contradicted by the image; Body size appears notably large and bulky for a cowbird — Bronzed Cowbird is visibly larger and more robust than Shiny Cowbird; Bill length appears moderate relative to head size — not an unusually long, slender, or finch-like bill; the depth-to-length ratio reinforces the heavy-based profile; No contrasting brown or buffy wingbar, edging, or underpart coloration visible — the bird is uniformly dark throughout, ruling out female or immature plumages of either species
action: Classify as Bronzed Cowbird. Confidence: high. Rule: When a wholly black cowbird shows a prominent red or ruby-red iris clearly visible in the photograph AND a distinctly thick-based, heavy, slightly decurved bill that appears noticeably deeper at the base than a typical icterid bill, identify as Bronzed Cowbird.
preconditions: Bird is entirely glossy black plumage (male plumage; no brown tones visible)
Iris is clearly red or ruby-red in color — not dark brown or black
Bill is visibly thick and heavy at the base, with a slightly decurved culmen, appearing proportionally deeper than a typical Shiny Cowbird bill
No obvious iridescent purple-blue gloss dominating the head and body (Shiny Cowbird shows strong blue-purple iridescence; Bronzed shows greener-bronze gloss)

Bird shows a distinctly inflated or ruff-like appearance around the neck and upper breast — the prominent neck ruff (hackles) characteristic of Bronzed Cowbird is visible or at least not contradicted by the image
Body size appears notably large and bulky for a cowbird — Bronzed Cowbird is visibly larger and more robust than Shiny Cowbird
Bill length appears moderate relative to head size — not an unusually long, slender, or finch-like bill; the depth-to-length ratio reinforces the heavy-based profile
No contrasting brown or buffy wingbar, edging, or underpart coloration visible — the bird is uniformly dark throughout, ruling out female or immature plumages of either species
rule_text: When a wholly black cowbird shows a prominent red or ruby-red iris clearly visible in the photograph AND a distinctly thick-based, heavy, slightly decurved bill that appears noticeably deeper at the base than a typical icterid bill, identify as Bronzed Cowbird.

condition: [Patch rule — bronzed_cowbird_vs_shiny_cowbird] Bird is an all-dark/blackish cowbird-sized icterid perched in a way that the head and bill profile are visible; Iris color is clearly red or orange-red (not dark brown or black); Bill appears distinctly heavy and thick-based with a deep culmen, noticeably stouter than a Shiny Cowbird's slender bill; Overall plumage is glossy blue-black (iridescent sheen visible in good light); Bird lacks any brownish or buffy tones in the plumage that would suggest a female or immature individual of another cowbird species; Head shape appears rounded to distinctly peaked or bushy due to the ruff of neck feathers characteristic of male Bronzed Cowbird, rather than smoothly rounded; No purple or violet iridescence dominates the plumage; gloss is blue-green or blue-black rather than the strong violet-purple sheen typical of Shiny Cowbird; Bill is relatively short and decurved compared to the body, not elongated or pointed; the thick base does not taper to a notably sharp fine tip as in Shiny Cowbird; Bird does not show a contrasting brown or dark-brown body with a blacker head that would suggest a female Shiny Cowbird or Brown-headed Cowbird
action: Classify as Bronzed Cowbird. Confidence: high. Rule: When a black cowbird shows a distinctly red or orange-red iris visible at normal photographic distance, combined with a noticeably thick-based, heavy bill (deeper culmen depth than a typical icterid), identify as Bronzed Cowbird.
preconditions: Bird is an all-dark/blackish cowbird-sized icterid perched in a way that the head and bill profile are visible
Iris color is clearly red or orange-red (not dark brown or black)
Bill appears distinctly heavy and thick-based with a deep culmen, noticeably stouter than a Shiny Cowbird's slender bill
Overall plumage is glossy blue-black (iridescent sheen visible in good light)
Bird lacks any brownish or buffy tones in the plumage that would suggest a female or immature individual of another cowbird species
Head shape appears rounded to distinctly peaked or bushy due to the ruff of neck feathers characteristic of male Bronzed Cowbird, rather than smoothly rounded
No purple or violet iridescence dominates the plumage; gloss is blue-green or blue-black rather than the strong violet-purple sheen typical of Shiny Cowbird
Bill is relatively short and decurved compared to the body, not elongated or pointed; the thick base does not taper to a notably sharp fine tip as in Shiny Cowbird
Bird does not show a contrasting brown or dark-brown body with a blacker head that would suggest a female Shiny Cowbird or Brown-headed Cowbird
rule_text: When a black cowbird shows a distinctly red or orange-red iris visible at normal photographic distance, combined with a noticeably thick-based, heavy bill (deeper culmen depth than a typical icterid), identify as Bronzed Cowbird.

condition: [Patch rule — bronzed_cowbird_vs_shiny_cowbird] Bird is entirely or nearly entirely dark/black-plumaged (male plumage); Bill appears thick, deep-based, and distinctly heavy or bulbous at the culmen base — noticeably heavier than a typical icterid bill; Head and nape appear large and rounded, with a puffed or ruffed neck creating a 'bull-necked' or top-heavy silhouette; Overall body shape looks compact and chunky rather than sleek and streamlined; No contrasting plumage patches (e.g., no brown or buff tones on head that would suggest a female Brown-headed Cowbird)
action: Classify as Bronzed Cowbird. Confidence: high. Rule: When a uniformly dark/black cowbird shows a visibly thick, deep-based, and conical bill that appears noticeably bulbous or heavy at the base (giving a 'bull-headed' profile), combined with a puffy or ruffed neck/nape that creates a rounded, large-headed silhouette, identify as Bronzed Cowbird.
preconditions: Bird is entirely or nearly entirely dark/black-plumaged (male plumage)
Bill appears thick, deep-based, and distinctly heavy or bulbous at the culmen base — noticeably heavier than a typical icterid bill
Head and nape appear large and rounded, with a puffed or ruffed neck creating a 'bull-necked' or top-heavy silhouette
Overall body shape looks compact and chunky rather than sleek and streamlined
No contrasting plumage patches (e.g., no brown or buff tones on head that would suggest a female Brown-headed Cowbird)
rule_text: When a uniformly dark/black cowbird shows a visibly thick, deep-based, and conical bill that appears noticeably bulbous or heavy at the base (giving a 'bull-headed' profile), combined with a puffy or ruffed neck/nape that creates a rounded, large-headed silhouette, identify as Bronzed Cowbird.

condition: [Patch rule — bronzed_cowbird_vs_shiny_cowbird] Bird is entirely or nearly entirely dark/black-plumaged with no contrasting brown or buff head patches; Head appears visibly oversized relative to the body — distinctly domed and wide, not compact and round like a typical icterid; Bill appears short and stubby, compressed vertically, lacking the fine tapered point typical of Shiny Cowbird's more slender bill; Overall silhouette reads as top-heavy or front-heavy rather than sleek and elongated; No visible wing bars, eye rings, or other contrasting plumage patches that would suggest a different species
action: Classify as Bronzed Cowbird. Confidence: high. Rule: When a uniformly black cowbird displays a disproportionately large, domed head that is visibly wider or taller than expected for the body size, combined with a short, stubby bill that appears compressed vertically and does not taper to a fine point, creating an overall top-heavy or 'big-headed' silhouette rather than a sleek, streamlined profile, identify as Bronzed Cowbird.
preconditions: Bird is entirely or nearly entirely dark/black-plumaged with no contrasting brown or buff head patches
Head appears visibly oversized relative to the body — distinctly domed and wide, not compact and round like a typical icterid
Bill appears short and stubby, compressed vertically, lacking the fine tapered point typical of Shiny Cowbird's more slender bill
Overall silhouette reads as top-heavy or front-heavy rather than sleek and elongated
No visible wing bars, eye rings, or other contrasting plumage patches that would suggest a different species
rule_text: When a uniformly black cowbird displays a disproportionately large, domed head that is visibly wider or taller than expected for the body size, combined with a short, stubby bill that appears compressed vertically and does not taper to a fine point, creating an overall top-heavy or 'big-headed' silhouette rather than a sleek, streamlined profile, identify as Bronzed Cowbird.
